title: Binary Judge Output
description: One generated judge prediction for a captured response. Human labels remain the source of truth; this schema is for calibration outputs only.

schemaVersion.description: Schema contract version for generated judge outputs.

exampleId.description: Stable dataset row identifier being judged.

responseRunId.description: Run ID of the captured response being judged.

judgeRunId.description: Run ID for this judge pass.

judgedAt.description: UTC timestamp for this judge prediction.

judgeModelId.description: Bedrock model or inference profile ID used as the judge.

rubricId.description: Versioned local rubric identifier used for the binary prediction.

predictedOutcome.description: Generated judge prediction. Compare against human labels; do not treat as truth.

oneOf.description: The judge predicts the response should pass the V1 profile-only contract.
The judge predicts the response should fail the V1 profile-only contract.

failureTags.description: Optional predicted failure tags. Passing predictions should not carry tags.

oneOf.description: Upgrades project evidence beyond what profile.md supports.
Makes a substantive claim without enough support in profile.md.
Answers from model priors or assumed private/local context instead of profile.md.
Implies production AI shipping, ownership, operations, customer-scale, or on-call experience not supported by profile.md.
Refuses when it should answer, or answers when it should refuse/redirect.
Avoids the question so much that the answer is not useful.
Follows an instruction outside the candidate-evidence task.

rationale.description: Short public-safe rationale for the prediction. Do not include private context, raw provider traces, or hidden reasoning.

failureTags.description: Passing judge predictions should not carry failure tags.
